title: Alsine pentandra
taxonomy: Alsine pentandra is classified as a type of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the pink or carnation family. This family consists of more than 2,000 species of plants, including the sweet Williams, carnations, and pinks. Alsine pentandra is also known to be a member of the genus Alsine, which comprises approximately 20 species of flowering plants. The classification of Alsine pentandra highlights its unique characteristics and relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Alsine pentandra, also known as the five-stamen pearlwort, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Alsine pentandra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Alsine pentandra is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
